title: Tectono-sedimentary evolution of marine slope basins in the Chilean forearc (33°30′–36°50′S): Insights into their link with the subduction process
abstract: Connections between plate convergence and forearc basin development along central Chile's margin (33°30′–36°50′S) are examined using seismic data. The study identifies asymmetric half-graben structures on the slope controlled by faults that govern differential uplift and subsidence patterns. Three primary seismic sequences — basal pre-kinematic, intermediate syn-kinematic, and upper post-kinematic units — characterize the sedimentary architecture. Marine seismic reflection data reveal the structural controls on slope basin formation and evolution in relation to the ongoing subduction process.

location.description: Offshore central Chile between 33°30′S and 36°50′S, marine slope basin analysis

seismic_lines.name: MCS profiles along central Chile slope (33.5°–36.9°S)

data.name: Multichannel seismic reflection profiles, central Chile slope basins (33.5°–36.9°S)
data.description: Marine MCS reflection data from central Chile margin (33.5°–36.9°S) used for seismic stratigraphic analysis of slope basins and half-graben structures.

processing.summary: Marine seismic reflection data from the central Chile margin (33.5°–36.9°S) processed to characterize the tectono-sedimentary evolution of slope basins. Standard MCS processing applied for structural and stratigraphic interpretation. Three seismic sequences identified (pre-kinematic, syn-kinematic, post-kinematic). Asymmetric half-graben structures identified as principal slope basin controlling structures.
workflow: 7. Structural mapping of half-grabens and fault system
